name: Hetzner Cloud API
what_it_does: REST API for provisioning and managing cloud servers, load balancers, persistent volumes, private networks, and firewalls on Hetzner's European and US infrastructure.
use_cases: Provisioning low-cost cloud servers (VMs) for agent compute workloads in Europe or US
Automating infrastructure lifecycle via a clean, well-documented REST API
Deploying private networks and firewalls for secure multi-server architectures
Managing load balancers and volumes programmatically in CI/CD or agent pipelines
Running cost-sensitive workloads where AWS/GCP/Azure pricing is prohibitive
not_for: Workloads needing a global footprint beyond Europe and select US regions
Teams requiring managed Kubernetes beyond Hetzner's basic k3s offering
Compliance regimes requiring FedRAMP, HIPAA, or ISO 27001 on cloud infrastructure
Deep integration with AWS or GCP managed services
best_when: You need excellent price-performance on cloud VMs with a clean, predictable API, especially for European data residency requirements or cost-optimized compute
avoid_when: You need global multi-region presence beyond Europe and US-East/West, or require enterprise-grade managed services (databases, ML, etc.)

auth.notes: API token passed as Bearer in Authorization header; tokens are project-scoped (one token per Hetzner project), which provides implicit scope separation at the project level

pricing.paid_tiers: CX22 (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM) from ~€4.15/month; dedicated CPX series from €7.52/month; volumes from €0.0476/GB/month
pricing.notes: Among the best price-performance ratios in the cloud market; billing is hourly with monthly caps; strong value for European compute

performance.rate_limits: 3600 requests per hour per project token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent structured JSON error responses with 'code' and 'message' fields and machine-readable error codes (e.g., 'server_not_found') — well-suited for programmatic error handling
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server creation is not idempotent; most update/patch operations are idempotent; action-based resources return consistent results on retry
known_agent_gotchas: Server actions are asynchronous — agents must poll the action endpoint until status is 'success'
Rate limit of 3600 req/hour is per project token; bulk operations can approach this limit quickly
Server IDs are numeric integers — agents should store and reference by ID, not name
Deleting a server does not automatically delete attached volumes — agents must handle cleanup explicitly
No managed database offering — agents needing databases must self-manage or integrate with external services

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; tokens are project-scoped providing implicit isolation; no fine-grained permission scopes within a project; GDPR compliant; Hetzner is a German company with strong privacy practices
